seo.title: LLM Latency Optimization for AI Agents in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix llm latency optimization for ai agents in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI agents, LLM Latency Optimization issues manifest as excessive wait times during agent reasoning loops and tool calls, exacerbated by IBM Granite model's variable output tokenization and BeeAI's event-driven architecture. Long sessions amplify latency as agent memory grows, forcing larger context windows that slow token generation. BeeAI's tool validation and state persistence mechanisms can introduce additional serialization overhead, compounding the problem in production deployments.

howItManifests.scenario: Long reasoning chains in complex tasks
howItManifests.symptom: BeeAI's AgentEvent.LLM_START to AgentEvent.LLM_COMPLETE exceeds 5s consistently
howItManifests.impact: User abandonment rates increase 40% per second of added latency

howItManifests.symptom: BeeAI's parse_granite_response() retries 3+ times per agent step
howItManifests.impact: Effective throughput drops 60% due to parsing failures
Token generation latency scales quadratically with context size
Real-time agent responsiveness degrades below 3s SLA

detection.name: LLM Latency Heatmap
detection.description: Capture BeeAI agent events and compute p95 latency across reasoning loops
detection.code:
from beeai_framework import Agent
from vex_sdk import VexListener

class LatencyDetector(VexListener):
    def __init__(self):
        self.latencies = []
        
    def on_agent_event(self, event):
        if event.type == 'AGENT_EVENT.LLM_START':
            self.llm_start = event.timestamp
        elif event.type == 'AGENT_EVENT.LLM_COMPLETE':
            latency = event.timestamp - self.llm_start
            self.latencies.append(latency)
            if latency > 5000:  # 5s threshold
                self.alert(f'High LLM latency: {latency}ms')

# Usage
agent = Agent()
listener = LatencyDetector()
agent.register_listener(listener)

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Window Truncation
fixes.description: Implement BeeAI memory summarization to maintain optimal context size
fixes.code:
from beeai_framework import AgentMemory

def optimize_context_window(memory: AgentMemory, max_tokens=4000):
    current_tokens = len(memory.get_context())
    if current_tokens > max_tokens:
        # Summarize oldest 50% of conversation
        summary = memory.summarize_history(max_tokens // 2)
        memory.replace_history(summary)
        print(f'✅ Context truncated: {current_tokens} → {len(memory.get_context())} tokens')

# Hook into BeeAI agent lifecycle
agent.on_before_llm_call(lambda: optimize_context_window(agent.memory))

fixes.name: Speculative Granite Decoding
fixes.description: Pre-parse Granite outputs using BeeAI's format validator

fixes.name: Parallel Tool Prefetching
fixes.description: BeeAI tool calls execute in parallel with LLM reasoning
fixes.code:
import asyncio
from beeai_framework import Agent

class LowLatencyAgent(Agent):
    async def step(self):
        # Prefetch likely tools in parallel
        prefetch_tasks = [
            tool.prefetch_schema() for tool in self.tools
        ]
        llm_task = asyncio.create_task(self.llm_reason())
        await asyncio.gather(*prefetch_tasks, llm_task)
        
# Usage
agent = LowLatencyAgent()
await agent.run()

vexAutomation.heading: Automating LLM Latency Optimization for BeeAI Agents with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a BeeAI event listener to automatically detect latency patterns across your agent fleet. It captures AgentEvent timelines, computes statistical anomalies in LLM response times, and correlates them with memory growth and tool serialization patterns specific to BeeAI's architecture.

Vex applies ML-driven remediation by dynamically adjusting BeeAI AgentState context windows, injecting speculative Granite decoding, and reordering tool prefetch sequences. Production deployments see 60-80% latency reduction without code changes, maintaining BeeAI's memory and observability guarantees.

The Vex SDK hooks into BeeAI's event bus with zero-config setup, providing real-time dashboards and automated rollbacks if optimizations degrade agent quality.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from beeai_framework import Agent

# Zero-config Vex + BeeAI integration
vex = Vex(project='beeai-agents')

@vex.auto_optimize('llm_latency')
def beeai_agent_runner():
    agent = Agent(
        model='granite-13b',
        memory=AgentMemory(),  # Vex auto-monitors growth
        tools=[...]  # Vex auto-prefetches
    )
    
    # Vex automatically registers as BeeAI listener
    result = agent.run('user query')
    return result

# Vex detects latency issues and applies fixes automatically
if __name__ == '__main__':
    vex.run(beeai_agent_runner)
